native_name: ปาเลอุง (ตาอาง)
textile_history: Palaung women are known for their distinctive woven skirts with horizontal bands of red, black, and yellow, often featuring small geometric motifs. The rattan bands worn around the waist are a cultural signature.
cultural_significance: The horizontal band pattern on Palaung skirts represents the layers of the cosmos in their animist worldview. Specific combinations indicate sub-group identity (Palaung, Ruchang, Rumai).
